name: Lugh
description: Lugh, also known as Lugh Lámhfhada (Lugh of the Long Arm), is a prominent god in Irish mythology. He is associated with skill, craftsmanship, the arts, and warfare. Lugh's parentage is unique, being the son of Cian of the Tuatha Dé Danann and Ethniu (or Ethliu), daughter of Balor of the Fomorians. This lineage placed him between the two warring factions, a position that ultimately allowed him to bring peace and prosperity to the Tuatha Dé Danann. He is often depicted as a youthful and radiant figure, embodying the qualities of a solar deity. Lugh is celebrated for his mastery of all arts and crafts, earning him the title 'Samildánach' (skilled in all arts). His arrival among the Tuatha Dé Danann was initially met with skepticism, but his unparalleled skills quickly proved his worth. He played a pivotal role in the Second Battle of Mag Tuired, where he led the Tuatha Dé Danann to victory against the Fomorians, slaying his grandfather Balor with a sling stone that pierced his all-seeing eye. Lugh's reign brought a golden age to Ireland, marked by peace, prosperity, and artistic achievement. He is a symbol of innovation, skill, and the triumph of light over darkness, and is revered as one of the greatest heroes of Irish mythology, a polymathic figure whose influence is felt across various aspects of life and culture. His festival, Lughnasadh, marks the beginning of the harvest season, further cementing his connection to abundance and prosperity.
subtitle: The Many-Skilled God of Light
achievements: Leading the Tuatha Dé Danann to victory

keyMyths.title: Lugh's Arrival
keyMyths.description: Lugh arrived at the court of Nuada and impressed the gatekeeper by claiming to be skilled in every art. He proved his worth and was admitted, displaying his exceptional talents.

keyMyths.title: The Slaying of Balor
keyMyths.description: During the Second Battle of Mag Tuired, Lugh confronted his grandfather, Balor, and killed him with a sling stone to the eye. This victory secured the Tuatha Dé Danann's dominance.

keyMyths.title: The Festival of Lughnasadh
keyMyths.description: Lugh established the festival of Lughnasadh in honor of his foster mother, Tailtiu, who died clearing the plains of Ireland for agriculture. The festival celebrates the harvest and Lugh's reign.

sources.author: John Arnott MacCulloch
sources.work: The Religion of the Ancient Celts
sources.description: A comprehensive overview of Celtic religion and mythology.
sources.text: A comprehensive overview of Celtic religion and mythology.
